title: DECEASED OR DISABLED PUBLIC SAFETY OFFICER GRANT - 211info
text: HIGHER EDUCATION COORDINATING COMMISSION DECEASED OR DISABLED PUBLIC SAFETY OFFICER GRANT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A grant supporting dependents of public safety officers who were killed or disabled in the line of duty. Applicant must not have achieved a baccalaureate or higher degree from any post secondary institution. Awards are made on an annual basis and may be received for a maximum equivalent to four academic years (up to twelve quarters or eight semesters), or until a bachelor's degree is earned, whichever comes first. 3225 25th Street SE Salem, OR 97302 Eligibility: -Be a dependent (natural child, adopted child or stepchild) of a public safety officer in the state of Oregon who suffered death or permanent total disability suffered by a public safety officer while on or off duty -Be under 25 years of age at the time of submitting the application -Be an Oregon resident for at least 12 months -Not have achieved a baccalaureate or higher degree from any post secondary institution Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 687-7400 2 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 687-7400 ext 2 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Visit website, call, or email for more information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: State of Oregon Services: Student Financial Aid Other Services Offered At This Location: EDUCATIONAL SUPPORT SERVICES - CHAFEE EDUCATION AND TRAINING VOUCHER PROGRAM EDUCATIONAL SUPPORT SERVICES - OFFICE OF STUDENT ACCESS AND COMPLETION EDUCATIONAL SUPPORT SERVICES - OREGON BARBER AND HAIRDRESSER GRANT PROGRAM EDUCATIONAL SUPPORT SERVICES - OREGON OPPORTUNITY GRANT EDUCATIONAL SUPPORT SERVICES - OREGON PROMISE GRANT EDUCATIONAL SUPPORT SERVICES - OREGON TEACHER SCHOLARS PROGRAM GRANT EDUCATIONAL SUPPORT SERVICES - OREGON TRIBAL STUDENT GRANT MILITARY SERVICE - OREGON NATIONAL GUARD STATE TUITION ASSISTANCE PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - OREGON STUDENT CHILD CARE GRANT If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. HIGHER EDUCATION COORDINATING COMMISSION
